explicit_text: I, Enoch Percy French, have been a timber cruiser since about 1900. I was born in Gruerneville, California, in 1882, and my family moved to Scotia in 1889 and then to Rio Dell. My father was a lumberman who worked for the Pacific Lumber Company, and I learned the trade from him, starting as a young boy feeding oxen in the logging camps. By my late teens, I was running compass and cruising timber myself, eventually working on important projects like the Rockefeller Forest.

explicit_text: My family moved to Scotia, California, in December 1889, and it became central to our lives, especially since my father worked for the Pacific Lumber Company there. It was also where I began running compass with my father and later started my own timber cruising career.

explicit_text: I was involved in timber cruising for the Rockefeller Forest, which was part of the Pacific Lumber Company's holdings. Around 1930, the State Park Commission was negotiating to buy this forest, and I helped with the timber cruises that informed those deals. The Rockefeller Forest is a significant and well-known redwood area that I had the privilege to work with during my career.

My work contributed to understanding and managing these famous redwood groves.

It was in California, particularly around Scotia and Rio Dell, that I grew up and developed my career in the lumber industry. The state's vast forests and booming timber business shaped my life and work as a timber cruiser.
